name: Marrakech
description: The Red City enchants with its bustling medina, vibrant souks, and the iconic Jemaa el-Fnaa square. A sensory feast of colors, spices, and sounds.
bestTime: March to May, September to November
experiences: Cooking class in a traditional riad
Hot air balloon ride at sunrise
Motorcycle tour of the Atlas Mountains

details.intro: Marrakech is Morocco at full volume: lantern-lit souks, garden courtyards, rooftop dinners, and the constant theatre of Jemaa el-Fnaa. It is a brilliant base for first-time visitors because culture, food, design, nightlife, and Atlas Mountain day trips are all within easy reach.

areas.title: Medina
areas.description: Best for souks, riads, street food, and historic monuments.

areas.title: Gueliz
areas.description: Modern cafes, galleries, concept stores, and easier taxi access.

areas.title: Palmeraie
areas.description: Quiet resort stays, spa days, camel rides, and palm-grove escapes.

food: Orange juice and grilled brochettes near Jemaa el-Fnaa
localTips: Book popular riads early in spring and autumn.
Use fixed meeting points near the medina because cars cannot enter many lanes.
Visit gardens early to avoid heat and tour groups.
details.gettingAround: Walk inside the medina, use petit taxis for Gueliz and garden visits, and book private transport for Ourika Valley or Agafay Desert.
details.whereToStay: Stay inside the medina for atmosphere, Gueliz for easier logistics, or Palmeraie for a resort-style retreat.

seo.title: Marrakech
seo.description: The Red City enchants with its bustling medina, vibrant souks, and the iconic Jemaa el-Fnaa square. A sensory feast of colors, spices, and sounds.

name: Chefchaouen
description: The Blue Pearl of Morocco is a photographer's paradise with its striking blue-washed buildings nestled in the Rif Mountains.
bestTime: April to June, September to October

details.intro: Chefchaouen is calm, blue, and beautifully slow. Tucked into the Rif Mountains, it rewards wandering more than checklist sightseeing: quiet stairways, mountain viewpoints, craft shops, and long mint-tea pauses.

areas.title: Old Medina
areas.description: Blue alleys, small guesthouses, craft shops, and classic photo corners.

areas.title: Spanish Mosque Trail
areas.description: The best sunset viewpoint over the blue city.

areas.title: Akchour
areas.description: Waterfalls and hiking trails for a nature-focused day trip.

food: Tagines served on quiet terrace restaurants
localTips: Stay overnight; day trips miss the best morning and evening light.
Wear comfortable shoes because the medina is steep.
details.gettingAround: The medina is best explored on foot. For Akchour, arrange a taxi or guided transfer from town.
details.whereToStay: Choose a small guesthouse in the old medina for views, charm, and easy sunrise walks.

seo.title: Chefchaouen
seo.description: The Blue Pearl of Morocco is a photographer's paradise with its striking blue-washed buildings nestled in the Rif Mountains.

name: Sahara Desert
description: Experience the magic of the world's largest hot desert. Sleep under a canopy of stars in a luxury desert camp and ride camels across golden dunes.

details.intro: The Sahara is less about ticking off a place and more about the transition into silence: kasbah roads, fossil towns, dunes at golden hour, Berber music by firelight, and a sky that feels impossibly close.

areas.title: Erg Chebbi
areas.description: The classic Merzouga dune experience with dramatic sand ridges.

areas.title: Todra Gorge
areas.description: A scenic canyon stop often paired with Sahara routes.

areas.title: Dades Valley
areas.description: Kasbahs, red cliffs, and winding mountain roads.

food: Camp dinners with harira and tagine
localTips: Pack warm layers from October to March; desert nights get cold.
Choose at least 3 days from Marrakech to avoid a rushed drive.
Bring a small overnight bag for camp stays.
details.gettingAround: A private driver or organized tour is the smoothest way to reach the dunes, especially from Marrakech or Fes.
details.whereToStay: Pick a desert camp near the dunes for sunset access, or a kasbah hotel if you prefer more comfort and shorter transfers.

seo.title: Sahara Desert
seo.description: Experience the magic of the world's largest hot desert. Sleep under a canopy of stars in a luxury desert camp and ride camels across golden dunes.

name: Fes
description: Morocco's cultural and spiritual heart, home to the world's oldest university and the largest car-free urban area. A living museum of medieval Islamic architecture.
bestTime: March to May, September to November

details.intro: Fes is Morocco’s intellectual and craft heart, dense with history and best explored with context. Its medina feels like a living archive: tanneries, madrasas, bakeries, brass workshops, and family-run food stalls.

areas.title: Fes el-Bali
areas.description: The old medina and the core of the city’s craft and religious heritage.

areas.title: Tanneries
areas.description: Iconic leather workshops best visited with a local guide.

areas.title: Mellah
areas.description: Historic Jewish quarter with different architecture and quieter streets.

localTips: Hire a licensed guide for the first medina day.
Expect narrow lanes and limited GPS accuracy.
Visit major monuments in the morning for calmer viewing.
details.gettingAround: Walk inside the medina and use taxis between the medina gates, Ville Nouvelle, and viewpoints.
details.whereToStay: A restored riad near a main medina gate gives atmosphere without difficult luggage logistics.

seo.title: Fes
seo.description: Morocco's cultural and spiritual heart, home to the world's oldest university and the largest car-free urban area. A living museum of medieval Islamic architecture.

name: Essaouira
description: A laid-back coastal town with Portuguese fortifications, artistic vibes, fresh seafood, and some of Africa's best windsurfing conditions.

details.intro: Essaouira is Morocco’s breezy Atlantic reset: white-and-blue lanes, sea walls, artists’ studios, fresh fish grills, and a beach culture shaped by wind, music, and long sunsets.

areas.title: Medina
areas.description: Art shops, cafes, guesthouses, and easy walks to the ramparts.

areas.title: Fishing Port
areas.description: Fresh seafood, blue boats, and the city’s working coastal rhythm.

areas.title: Sidi Kaouki
areas.description: A wilder beach south of town for surf days and open skies.

localTips: Bring a windbreaker even in summer.
Choose morning beach time before the wind picks up.
Essaouira pairs well with Marrakech as a 2-3 day contrast.
details.gettingAround: The medina and beach are walkable. Use taxis or transfers for Sidi Kaouki and nearby villages.
details.whereToStay: Stay in the medina for atmosphere or near the beach for surf schools and ocean views.

seo.title: Essaouira
seo.description: A laid-back coastal town with Portuguese fortifications, artistic vibes, fresh seafood, and some of Africa's best windsurfing conditions.
